scope_boundary: Public-safe local external Circle recurrence-schedule receipt import only. The slice records one pinned `CC-AI-CONTRACT-RECURRENCE-001` structural fixture at Circle commit `63b0f511`, active/inactive token work traces, scheduled work-saving fields, whole-period shift invariants, three required theorem IDs, two planner recommendations, contract fingerprint `571edd5dce4f7b64441806de323295218a3e2293b3b540dd4772ba34b9371515`, command-output digests, and a passing Circle CLI test summary. It does not prove deployed recurrence behavior, reasoning quality, retrieval quality, learned-memory behavior, convergence, model quality, long-context utility, transfer, safety, ASI, or Coil chapter-core promotion.
transition_reason: The recurrence receipt slice makes one Circle recurrence-schedule structural fixture concrete in the book and closes the stale 'no recurrence certifier import' blocker for the Coil chapter. The evidence remains an external-project receipt import with no deployed ASI-side recurrence-controller trace, no ordinary attention or recurrence baseline, no memory-quality workload, no long-context result, no model-quality result, and no independent review, so it blocks promotion rather than moving support state.
required_artifacts: fresh public Circle replay or archived public recurrence contract pack
repository-owned command that reruns the relevant Circle recurrence proof target or verifies an archived replay bundle
deployed or replayed ASI-side recurrence-controller trace
ordinary attention and recurrence baselines with workload metrics
retrieval-quality, reasoning-quality, long-context, convergence, memory-savings, and model-quality measurements for any promoted claim
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: no deployed recurrence behavior is claimed
no reasoning quality, retrieval quality, learned-memory, convergence, long-context, model-quality, transfer, safety, or ASI claim is promoted
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: active-work, saved-work, or shift-invariant evidence field drift
external-project receipt import described as deployed recurrence behavior
reasoning-quality, retrieval-quality, learned-memory, convergence, long-context, model-quality, transfer, safety, ASI, or support-state promotion claimed without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed recurrence-schedule claim after a fresh public Circle replay or archived public contract pack, repository-owned proof/replay verification, deployed or replayed ASI-side recurrence-controller traces, ordinary baselines, retrieval/reasoning/long-context/convergence/model-quality measurements for the specific claim, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted upward transition.
limitations: one pinned local external Circle receipt import
external Circle checkout is not vendored into this public repository
no clean ASI-side CI replay of Circle
no reasoning-quality, retrieval-quality, learned-memory, convergence, long-context, model-quality, transfer, safety, or ASI measurement
does not promote any chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent Circle Calculus, recurrence-controller, memory-quality, long-context, benchmark, model-quality, transfer, or safety audit
acceptance_blockers: no fresh public Circle replay inside this repository
no archived public Circle recurrence contract pack
no deployed or replayed ASI-side recurrence-controller trace
non_claims: does not promote Coil Attention or Circle recurrence evidence above argument

does not prove deployed recurrence behavior, reasoning quality, retrieval quality, learned-memory behavior, convergence, model quality, context length, speed, memory savings, deployment safety, transfer, benchmark performance, or ASI
